exercises.title: Write Your SMART Goals
exercises.description: Create 3 SMART goals for this quarter: one career goal, one learning goal, and one health goal. For each, verify it passes the SMART checklist: Is it Specific? Measurable? Achievable? Relevant? Time-bound? If any answer is no, revise it.
hints: Start with what you want, then work backward to make it SMART
Use numbers and dates — 'improve' is vague, 'complete 15 practice problems by March 31' is specific
Check achievability: do you have the time, resources, and skills to do this?

solution.code:
SMART GOALS EXAMPLES:

CAREER GOAL:
❌ 'Get promoted'
✅ 'Complete 3 high-impact projects (auth refactor, API v2, dashboard redesign)
    and receive positive 360 review feedback by June 30'
    S: 3 specific projects + 360 feedback
    M: 3 projects completed + positive feedback
    A: Projects are already planned in roadmap
    R: Directly supports senior promotion criteria
    T: June 30 deadline

LEARNING GOAL:
❌ 'Learn system design'
✅ 'Complete 15 system design practice problems on Pramp (score 7+),
    read 2 architecture books, and document 1 real system at work
    by April 30'
    S: Specific platform, books, and documentation task
    M: 15 problems + 2 books + 1 doc
    A: ~5 hours/week commitment, achievable
    R: Critical for career growth
    T: April 30 deadline

HEALTH GOAL:
❌ 'Exercise more'
✅ 'Run 3x/week (Mon/Wed/Fri) for 30 min before work, tracked in
    Strava, with a goal of running 10 miles/week by end of Q1'
    S: Running, 3x/week, specific days and times
    M: 10 miles/week, tracked in Strava
    A: 30 min sessions are realistic
    R: Improves focus, energy, and stress management
    T: End of Q1

exercises.title: Create Your Personal OKR
exercises.description: Choose one of your SMART goals and convert it into a personal OKR. Write one inspiring Objective and 2-4 quantitative Key Results. Then score yourself honestly at the end of the quarter: 0.0-0.3 (missed), 0.4-0.6 (partial), 0.7-1.0 (hit). Target 0.7 — that means you pushed yourself but didn't burn out.
hints: The Objective should be motivating — use inspiring language
Key Results should be numbers you can measure, not activities
If every Key Result is 1.0, your goals aren't ambitious enough

solution.code:
PERSONAL OKR EXAMPLE:

OBJECTIVE: Become a confident full-stack developer

KEY RESULTS:
• KR1: Complete 20 coding challenges on LeetCode (Easy+Medium)
       with solutions documented in my GitHub repo
       (0 → 20 challenges)
• KR2: Build and deploy 1 full-stack project (React + Node.js)
       with 90%+ test coverage, live at a public URL
       (0 → 1 deployed project)
• KR3: Write 4 technical blog posts about what I learned
       (0 → 4 posts published)

SCORING EXAMPLE:
• KR1: Completed 15/20 challenges → Score: 0.75
• KR2: Project deployed but only 75% test coverage → Score: 0.65
• KR3: Published 3/4 posts → Score: 0.75
• Overall OKR Score: 0.72

VERDICT: Strong quarter. Push harder on testing next time.

exercises.title: Habit Stack Builder
exercises.description: Design a habit stack that turns your goals into automatic daily behaviors. Identify 3 existing routines you do every day without thinking. Attach a new goal-related behavior to each one using the 'After I [existing habit], I will [new habit]' formula. Practice for 2 weeks and track completion rates.
hints: Choose existing habits that happen at consistent times (morning coffee, lunch, closing laptop)
Start with VERY small new habits (5 minutes, not 60 minutes)
Track completion — if you're below 80%, the habit is too big

solution.code:
HABIT STACK EXAMPLE:

EXISTING ROUTINE → NEW GOAL HABIT

1. After I pour morning coffee (7:30 AM)
   → I will review today's 3 priorities on my Notion board
   → Time: 2 minutes
   → Goal it supports: Career (focus on high-impact work)

2. After I sit at my desk (9:00 AM)
   → I will start a 25-minute pomodoro on my most important task
   → Time: 25 minutes
   → Goal it supports: Career (deep work discipline)

3. After I close laptop at 5:00 PM
   → I will change into workout clothes
   → Time: 3 minutes
   → Goal it supports: Health (exercise routine)

4. After I finish lunch (12:30 PM)
   → I will read 10 pages of a tech book
   → Time: 15 minutes
   → Goal it supports: Learning (system design knowledge)

5. After I brush teeth at night (10:00 PM)
   → I will write 3 things I'm grateful for today
   → Time: 2 minutes
   → Goal it supports: Wellness (stress management)

TRACKING (2 weeks):
| Habit       | Week 1 | Week 2 | Rate |
|-------------|--------|--------|------|
| Priorities  | 5/7    | 6/7    | 79%  |
| Pomodoro    | 4/7    | 5/7    | 64%  |
| Workout     | 3/7    | 4/7    | 50%  |
| Reading     | 6/7    | 6/7    | 86%  |
| Gratitude   | 5/7    | 7/7    | 86%  |
